description: RGB shows an urban/semi-urban scene with fields and roads; SWIR reveals a large, deep dark-blue region on the eastern side of the frame indicating extensive standing water consistent with active flooding.
labeler_notes: Floodwater signature in SWIR is unambiguous — dark blue covers roughly a third of the frame.

description: RGB shows a rural corridor with a road/channel and small settlement; SWIR shows large dark-blue water coverage dominating the right half and threading through linear channels, indicating widespread active flooding.
labeler_notes: Blue water signal in SWIR is extensive; consistent with peak flood or shortly after.

description: Both RGB and SWIR are entirely white with no ground visible; complete cloud cover prevents any surface interpretation.
labeler_notes: Total cloud cover — no usable signal in either band.

description: Both RGB and SWIR show near-total white cloud cover with only faint traces in SWIR; ground is fully obscured.
labeler_notes: Effectively same cloud situation as o0 for this timestamp.

description: RGB is dominated by white clouds with only a thin diagonal strip of dark ground visible at the lower portion; SWIR shows the same clouds as white with a narrow bright-green vegetation strip, too limited for hazard assessment.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover exceeds ~85% of frame; the small ground strip is insufficient for meaningful labeling.

description: RGB shows mostly white cloud cover with scattered dark wisps; SWIR similarly cloud-dominated with only small green ground patches, preventing confident surface assessment.
labeler_notes: Cloud fraction approximately 80-90%; only fragments of ground visible.

description: RGB shows a dense urban grid with dark surfaces and roads; SWIR shows a yellow-green and magenta pattern consistent with dense urban development, with no discernible dark water bodies indicating flood inundation.
labeler_notes: Clear cloud-free urban scene; no flood signature visible in SWIR at this timestamp despite event window.

description: RGB shows suburban/urban development in the lower half and forested area above; SWIR shows bright-green healthy vegetation and magenta urban grid with no significant dark water pooling visible.
labeler_notes: Cloud-free; SWIR does not reveal flood inundation in this tile at this date.

description: RGB has roughly 40-50% patchy cloud cover obscuring urban ground; SWIR similarly patchy with clouds and urban signal through gaps, materially limiting interpretation.
labeler_notes: Borderline cloud fraction; called SKIP because cloud patchwork prevents confident assessment of flood extent.

description: RGB shows ~50% patchy cloud cover with urban and vegetated patches visible through gaps; SWIR also cloud-patchy with green ground tones, insufficient for reliable hazard assessment.
labeler_notes: Similar cloud fraction to o0; ground visible but fragmented — too uncertain for a confident priority label.
